one_liner: Polaris Health offers asynchronous mental health intake to small employer plans.
use_of_funds_summary: Complete state licensure expansion and hire one clinical operations lead.
transcript_text: Polaris Health provides asynchronous mental health intake for small employer plans. We launched 9 months ago and currently serve 6 employer customers covering approximately 4,200 lives. Engagement, defined as completing the intake assessment within 30 days of activation, was 28 percent across the last cohort. Average revenue per covered life is 4.20 dollars per month. Gross margin is 62 percent based on Q3 clinical staff and platform costs. We are licensed in 4 states today; the 200,000 dollar check funds expansion to 6 additional states and one clinical operations lead. State licensure for mental health services has known regulatory complexity, and our compliance review is in progress with our regulatory counsel. Our team includes a licensed clinical psychologist, a former employer benefits sales lead, and two senior engineers. Outcome data is encouraging but the cohort is still small; we are not claiming clinical efficacy and our marketing materials reflect that. Customer acquisition cost on the employer side averaged 6,800 dollars over the last quarter, with payback of approximately 14 months.
